_comment: Epic N (ADR 0035) N-S3 — C++ namespace + `using` + cross-file namespace resolution on the tree-sitter floor (ceiling `likely`). A function inside `namespace ns { }` is qualified `ns::f` (decl pass treats the namespace as an enclosing scope, like a class in N-S1), accumulating through nesting (`outer::inner::deep`). A bare call from WITHIN the namespace resolves enclosing-first: `side()` inside `geometry::area` -> `geometry::side`. A SCOPED call binds to the declaration whose node id is the call's FULL `::`-path: `compute -> geometry::area`, `main -> geometry::side`, and `useDeep -> outer::inner::deep` (NOT the unrelated `inner::deep` of `struct inner` — full-path match, no 2-segment collision). A scoped call whose target is NOT in this file binds CROSS-FILE to the unique same-directory namespace-member definition: `useRemote -> lib.cpp#remote::fetch` (the N-S2 dir-as-module proxy extended to namespace members; class members are NOT indexed; ambiguous/cross-dir stays `unresolved`). Only functions whose qualified name comes from their ENCLOSING scope are indexed — an OUT-OF-LINE member definition (`int Gadget::spin(){}` at file scope) is NOT a cross-file target, so `useGadget`'s `Gadget::spin()` stays `unresolved` (conservative: never over-resolve a member call). CROSS-DIRECTORY: a same-dir miss binds via the `#include` graph — `ns.cpp` `#include "sub/deep.h"`, whose sibling source `sub/deep.cpp` defines `farns::compute`, so `useFar -> sub/deep.cpp#farns::compute` resolves across the `sub/` boundary (only when exactly one include-connected file defines the name). `using` declarations resolve bare calls: `using geometry::side;` makes `viaUsing`'s bare `side()` -> `geometry::side`, and `using namespace text;` makes bare `helper()` -> `text::helper` (file-scoped approximation; bound only when the member is declared in-file; >1 used namespace declaring a name stays `unresolved`). CONFLICTING using-declarations (`using one::dup;` + `using two::dup;`) are AMBIGUOUS, so `useDup`'s bare `dup()` stays `unresolved` — never a guess. `text::helper` is unreachable from `main`. An unknown scope/name stays `unresolved`. OVER-RESOLUTION regressions (`edges.cpp`, from the ADR 0035 adversarial audit — a false edge is worse than `unresolved`): a `.`/`->` member call (`conn->send()`, `conn.send()`) stays `unresolved`, never binds to a same-named free function; a constructor `Box()` is NOT a call edge to the `Box` class node; an explicit-global `::glob()` binds to the free `glob`, never the same-named member `Holder::glob`; an OUT-OF-LINE member body `Svc2::run2(){ step(); }` resolves `step()` to the member `Svc2::step` (member-first via the restored class scope), never the same-named free `step()`; and an ANONYMOUS-namespace function (`lib.cpp`'s `anonFn`, internal linkage) is never resolvable cross-file, so `useAnon`'s `anonFn()` stays `unresolved`; and a bare call to a DECLARED-but-not-defined member (`Svc3::run3(){ poke(); }` where `Svc3::poke` is declared in-class but never defined) stays `unresolved`, never over-resolving to the same-named free `poke()` (this holds for a reference-returning member too — `RefHolder::run4(){ acquire(); }` with `int& acquire()` declared-only stays `unresolved`, never the value-returning free `acquire()`); and a bare call to a PARAMETER or LOCAL that shadows a free function (`invoke(void(*handle)())` → `handle()` through the fn-pointer param; `runCb(){ std::function<...> tickCb; tickCb(); }`; `useFunctor(){ Functor doit; doit(); }`) is an indirect/`dynamic` dispatch through the local, graded `unclear` and left `unresolved`, never bound to the same-named free function.
